message:
perf(pager): wire bumpalo arena into materialize_retained_memory_overlay (OPT-5)

First caller of the per-transaction bumpalo arena (IMPL-3 / AG-4B). The
`materialize_retained_memory_overlay_into_write_set` method allocates a
transient `Vec<PageNumber>` of overlay-dirty pages, filters it, then
consumes it via `.iter().copied()` before returning. Classic fit for the
arena: the buffer is born in the method, read in the method, never
escapes.

Conservative lifetime analysis in the code comment: PageNumber is Copy,
no reference to arena-allocated storage escapes, arena-borrow ends via
an explicit `drop(overlay_page_nos)` before the `&mut self` reacquisition
for `insert_staged_page`.

Also: enable `bumpalo` `collections` feature so we can use
`bumpalo::collections::Vec::new_in(arena)`.

Net effect: one glibc `malloc(capacity * sizeof(PageNumber))` per
retained-autocommit commit converts to a bump-pointer reservation.
The arena resets for free at commit/rollback. Removes the `dead_code`
allow on `scratch_arena()` — now genuinely live.

Expected savings: very small — the malloc in question is rare (one per
commit boundary on retained-autocommit private-memory). The broader
goal of OPT-5 (retire some of the 9.6% glibc malloc self-time) needs
more sites wired; this commit ships the first safe site as proof of
pattern.

Agent-generated via rate-limited run; recovered as a hand-commit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
